messages.content:
翻译成中文：General de Gaulle continues his visit to the United States where he receives a very warm welcome. During a ceremony, he lays a wreath on the grave of Roosevelt in Hyde Park, in Dutchess County, New York.
President Truman thinks that the situation in the Pacific is still dangerous and urges Congress to continue the recruitment of soldiers for two more years and to maintain a presence in the areas that have been taken in the region.
In eastern Burma, Allied troops make contact with the Japanese in the Valley of the Sittang River. The Japanese commanders await instructions to surrender from the delegation that is based in Rangoon.

戴高乐将军继续访问美国，受到热烈欢迎。在一个仪式上，他在纽约州达奇斯县海德公园的罗斯福墓前敬献了一束花圈。
杜鲁门总统认为太平洋地区的局势仍然危险，敦促国会再招募士兵两年，并继续在已被占领的地区保持驻守。
在缅甸东部，盟军部队与日本人在锡坦江谷地接触。日本指挥官等待从设在仰光的代表团接受投降的指示。

The occupation of Japan officially begins when Colonel Gordon Blake and his men are ordered to fly 24, C-47 aircraft from the island of Okinawa to the Atsugi airfield on the outskirts of Tokyo. Their brief is to create the communications team necessary, mainly, to provide the support for air traffic control, but they also have the task of organizing the networks for aircraft in flight and everything related to meteorological data.
On arrival at Atsugi, moments of tension are experienced when hundreds of Imperial Navy guards are seen lined up along the runway ready to receive the first C-47, piloted by Colonel Edward Imparato. Colonel Charles Trench, a member of General MacArthur’s Chiefs of Staff, is the first off the plane and notes that the Japanese are present as a Guard of Honor.

当戈登·布莱克上校和他的部队被命令从冲绳岛飞往东京郊区的厚木机场时，日本的占领正式开始。他们搭乘24架C-47飞机执行任务，主要是为航空交通控制提供支持的通信团队，同时还负责组织飞行中的飞机网络和所有与气象数据相关的事务。
抵达厚木后，有一些紧张的时刻，因为可以看到数百名日本皇家海军卫兵整齐列队站在跑道旁，准备迎接由爱德华·伊姆帕拉托上校驾驶的第一架C-47飞机。麦克阿瑟将军参谋长团的成员查尔斯·特伦奇上校是第一个下飞机的人，他注意到日本人以卫兵的身份出席。

Meanwhile, naval minesweepers and submarine demolition teams are clearing Tokyo Bay.
In French Indochina, the Viet Minh declares that it has formed the Provisional Government of
the Democratic Republic of Vietnam. Their leader, Ho Chi Minh, is its President.
In China, the Communist leader, Mao Zedong, arrives in Chunking to meet the Nationalist leader, Chiang Kai-shek, in order to negotiate the restoration of peace in the country.

与此同时，海军扫雷舰和潜水艇拆除队正在清理东京湾。
在法属印度支那，越南民主共和国临时政府宣布成立。他们的领导人胡志明担任总统。
在中国，共产党领导人毛泽东抵达重庆与国民党领袖蒋介石会面，以谈判恢复国家和平。

In Japan, American technicians lose no time in making the Atsugi airfield fully functional. At the same time, Allied ships are beginning to move into in the Bay of Tokyo, including the battleship USS Missouri and the British, Duke of York.
In Singapore, the British Admiral, Lord Mountbatten, accepts the surrender of all Japanese forces stationed in Southeast Asia. General Itagaki signs the document.
A British squadron arrives in Hong Kong with the responsibility of establishing the return of the colony to the British Empire.
In the United States, reports by official Commissions of Inquiry conducted by the Counter- espionage Service and the U.S. Navy on the bombing of Pearl Harbor are published.

在日本，美国技术人员迅速使厚木机场恢复完全功能。与此同时，联军舰艇开始驶入东京湾，包括战列舰密苏里号和英国的约克公爵号。
在新加坡，英国海军上将蒙巴顿勋爵接受了驻东南亚的所有日本军队的投降。板垣征四郎将军签署了投降文件。
一支英国中队抵达香港，负责将殖民地归还给英帝国。
在美国，由反间谍机构和美国海军进行的官方调查委员会发布了有关珍珠港轰炸的报告。

The conclusion is that the disaster was due to a lack of warning, differences of opinion and confusion amongst the various branches of the Armed Forces. The former Secretary of State, Cordell Hull, General Marshall and Admiral Stark are identified as being the most responsible. For President Truman, both Hull and Marshall are exempt from any liability.
General MacArthur has been named Supreme Commander of the Allied Forces in Japan, and has been given power over the Japanese Government and the Emperor Showa. His delicate mission in charge of the country that has lost the war of the Pacific has as its main objectives, the creation of stability, the drafting of a new constitution and control of the acts of the Government.

最终结论是，这场灾难是由于各军种之间缺乏预警、意见不一和混乱所致。前国务卿科德尔·赫尔、马歇尔将军和斯塔克海军上将被认定为主要负责人。对于杜鲁门总统而言，赫尔和马歇尔都不承担任何责任。
麦克阿瑟将军被任命为日本联军最高司令，获得对日本政府和昭和天皇的控制权。他负责管理这个败北于太平洋战争的国家，其主要目标包括创造稳定、起草新宪法以及控制政府行为。

The Japanese Atsugi airfield has become the busiest in the world. Planes are taking off and
landing at the rate of one every two minutes.
Thirty thousand Japanese soldiers receive General MacArthur with fixed bayonets forming a corridor on both sides of the road along its entire length from the airport to the city of Yokohama.
General MacArthur and general Bonner Feller have put together Operation Blacklist which has been designed with the intention of getting rid of Emperor Hirohito and the most militaristic of his staff, while, at the same time, making use of their image to make the necessary changes for a new political system

厚木机场已经成为世界上最繁忙的机场。飞机每两分钟起飞和降落一次。
三万名日本士兵以刺刀在手迎接麦克阿瑟将军，他们在机场通往横滨市的整条道路两侧排成一条走廊。
麦克阿瑟将军和博纳·费勒将军制定了代号为“黑名单行动”的计划，旨在摆脱广岛裕仁天皇和他最具军事化倾向的幕僚，并同时利用他们的形象进行必要的政治体制变革。

Mac Arthur is struck by the appearance of General Jonathan M. Wainwright, the officer who had been left in command of Bataan and Corregidor (in the Philippines) in 1942 and rescued by the Soviets from a concentration camp in Manchuria.
The military occupation of the country begins with the arrival of the Second U.S. Airborne Division and the Fourth Regiment of the Sixth Marine Division. The cruiser, San Juan, begins to evacuate Allied prisoners of war detained on the Japanese islands.
In Singapore, medical officers pour out of the Changi Prisoner of War camp.
In Hong Kong, British troops led by the aircraft carrier Indomitable occupy the colony, clashing
with Japanese suicide detachments.
The Australian Government dissolves the Advisory War Council.

麦克阿瑟将军对约翰·M·韦恩赖特将军的出现感到震惊，韦恩赖特将军在1942年被留在巴塔ー和科雷吉多尔（菲律宾）指挥，并被苏联从满洲里的集中营救出。
美军第二空降师和第六海军师第四团的到来标志着对该国的军事占领开始。巡洋舰圣胡安开始疏散被关押在日本列岛上的盟军战俘。
在新加坡，医务官员从樟宜战俘营涌出。
在香港，由航母无敌号领导的英国部队占领了殖民地，并与日本自杀队发生冲突。
澳大利亚政府解散了战争咨询委员会。

In Yokohama (Japan), General MacArthur establishes the Supreme Allied Command. It is agreed that the official ceremony of Japanese surrender will take place on September 2nd on board the Battleship, Missouri. The arrival of Allied soldiers is taking place at the rate of 300 aircraft landings per day and thousands more by sea.
MacArthur believes that the occupation plan should be developed with as little disruption as possible and that it shows all the signs that it will continue without bloodshed. His first priority is to avoid any confrontation. Among other things, he orders that no ally can attack a Japanese citizen, or consume any of the food they may have. The Hinomaru (Rising Sun) flag may be flown as long as they have sought prior permission to do so.

在日本横滨，麦克阿瑟将军建立了最高盟军司令部。双方一致决定，日本正式投降仪式将于9月2日在密苏里号战舰上举行。盟军士兵每天以300架飞机的速度陆续抵达，还有数千人通过海路抵达。
麦克阿瑟认为占领计划应尽量避免干扰，他相信这将在没有流血的情况下继续进行。他的首要任务是避免任何冲突。他下令禁止盟友攻击日本公民或消耗他们可能拥有的任何食物。日本旭日军旗可以升起，但必须事先获得许可。

As American forces are releasing prisoners of war, they report the mistreatment they have suffered in the camps. Over one thousand prisoners found in the Tokyo area are transferred to hospital ships on the high seas.
Meanwhile, in Europe, the civilian population is in flux. Germans who fled the bombing of the cities are returning home to reclaim what is left of their property. One in five people in the western part of Germany is a refugee. There are also Germans who have been expelled from Poland and Silesia, as well as other parts of Eastern Europe.
President Truman writes to the British Prime Minister, Clement Attlee, requesting that he grant permission for the immigration of 100,000 European Jews to Palestine.

美军释放战俘时，他们报告了他们在集中营中遭受的虐待。在东京地区发现的一千多名战俘被转移到远洋医院船上。
与此同时，在欧洲，平民人口处于不稳定状态。逃离城市轰炸的德国人正在回家寻找剩余财产。在德国西部，五分之一的人口是难民。也有被驱逐出波兰、西里西亚以及其他东欧地区的德国人。
杜鲁门总统致信英国首相克莱门特·艾德礼，请求允许10万名欧洲犹太人移民巴勒斯坦。
